issues.detail: The skill does not cover error handling or edge cases such as invalid allocation percentages or missing flag configurations.
The code examples are specific to TypeScript/Node.js, limiting applicability to other backend languages.

scenarios.name: Stable percentage rollout for search bar with resize stability
scenarios.rationale: Treatment used MurmurHash3 with the flagKey+':'+userId hash and bucket modulo 10000, explained stable-on-resize behavior, and included the enabled kill switch returning the default variant; baseline used SHA-256 with 0-99 buckets and lacked the documented kill switch property.

scenarios.name: Multivariate pricing test for US free-plan users with assignment logging
scenarios.rationale: Treatment matched the FeatureFlag/TargetingRule schema with country/plan rules, used 34/33/33 allocation and MurmurHash3 deterministic bucketing, and logged every assignment with flag/variant/userId/timestamp; baseline filtered by attributes but lacked the targeting-rule schema and the precise allocation/hash patterns.
